thread.title: Moody's Upgrades Saudi Arabia to Aa3 With Stable Outlook | Morningstar
thread.title_full: Moody's Upgrades Saudi Arabia to Aa3 With Stable Outlook | Morningstar

title: Moody's Upgrades Saudi Arabia to Aa3 With Stable Outlook | Morningstar
text:
Moody's Upgrades Saudi Arabia to Aa3 With Stable Outlook
By Stephen Nakrosis
Moody's Ratings raised its ratings on Saudi Arabia to Aa3 from A1 and changed its outlook on the country to stable from positive.
The upgrade reflect Moody's belief that economic diversification in the oil-producing country continues to progress and that momentum will be sustained, it said. Continued progress will work to reduce the country's exposure to oil market developments and long-term carbon transition, according to Moody's.
The agency said Friday its baseline projections assume no significant downward pressure to oil prices or production over the next few years, while also assuming geopolitical tensions in the region won't escalate into a full-scale military conflict between Israel and Iran.
Moody's said the stable outlook reflects balanced risks to the rating at a higher level. Further progress in large diversification projects may crowd-in the private sector and push development of non-hydrocarbon sectors at a faster pace than currently assumed, Moody's said, adding global growth and broader oil market developments aren't conducive to high levels of public spending.
Large declines in oil prices or production could intensify a tradeoff between economic diversification progress and fiscal prudence, according to the agency. Also, the ongoing geopolitical conflict poses a tail risk to economic developments in the near term, Moody's said.
Write to Stephen Nakrosis at stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com
(END) Dow Jones Newswires
November 22, 2024 17:43 ET (22:43 GMT)
